ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metropolitan_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metropolitan status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the metropolitan status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metropolitan status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "ed_code" INTEGER /* Education code for the education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "education_category" TEXT /* Category of the education level (e.g., high school, college) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ed_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_classes" (
  "job_class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job class */,
  "job_class_name" TEXT /* Name of the job class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job class */,
  "job_category" TEXT /* Category of the job class (e.g., government, private) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Region where the individual resides */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Metropolitan status of the individual's residence */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Gender of the individual */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Marital status of the individual */,
  "ed_code" INTEGER /* Education level of the individual */,
  "job_class_id" INTEGER /* Job classification of the individual */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "weekly_earnings" REAL /* Weekly earnings of the individual */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the individual (e.g., employed, unemployed) */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the individual */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the individual */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES metropolitan_statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_job_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_class_id") REFERENCES job_classes ("job_class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_ed_code FOREIGN KEY ("ed_code") REFERENCES education_levels ("ed_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_statuses ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "earnings_data" (
  "earning_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each earnings record */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual */,
  "weekly_earnings" REAL /* Weekly earnings of the individual */,
  "record_date" TEXT /* Date the earnings record was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("earning_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_earnings_data_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual's data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data provided by the source (e.g., demographic, earnings) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual's data being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was updated */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., demographic, earnings) */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_updates_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)
